topic: A student finds out their scholarship was meant for someone else
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp autumn air did little to settle the knot tightening in my chest. I sat by the window, the textbook open but unread, the sunlight illuminating dust motes dancing in the stillness. The official notification, stark and impersonal, lay crumpled on my desk. The words, once a source of soaring hope, now felt like shards of ice. I traced the elegant script with a fingertip, the texture of the paper rough beneath my touch. It wasn't just disappointment; it was a profound sense of having been misplaced, a sudden, dizzying shift in perspective. I stood up, pacing the small confines of my room, the floorboards cool beneath my bare feet. Why? The question echoed hollowly in the silence.

[story 2]

He stared at the acceptance letter, his jaw tight enough to crack. The weight of the realization pressed down on him, making his shoulders slump. He ran a hand through his already disheveled hair, the movement jerky and uncoordinated. He walked to the edge of the campus green, the vast expanse of manicured lawn offering no solace. The sounds of students laughing and distant traffic seemed amplified, grating against his strained quiet. He closed his eyes, breathing in deeply, trying to anchor himself, but the image of the scholarship recipient—someone else entirely—kept swirling behind his eyelids. He felt a strange, heavy emptiness where certainty used to reside.

[story 3]

"It was for Sarah," the dean had said, his voice smooth and utterly devoid of apology. The words hung in the air, heavy and undeniable. I kept replaying the conversation in my head, the way the light caught the sheen of sweat on my palms. I leaned against the cool brick of the library wall, the rough texture grounding me momentarily. My gaze drifted across the bustling quad, each hurried stride of a passing student feeling like a deliberate dismissal. A slow, almost imperceptible shake ran through my frame. I pulled my backpack tighter against my chest, the weight of the unused opportunity suddenly feeling like a leaden anchor dragging me down.
